title: CAT Exam Pattern 2026 Overview Table

detail.title: CAT Exam Pattern 2026 Overview Table
detail.short_info: CAT Exam Pattern 2026 is to have 68 questions in 120 minutes across 3 sections – VARC (24), DILR (22) & QA (22). Check detailed section-wise break-up, marking scheme (+3/-1), and latest updates here.

content.text: CAT Exam Pattern 2026 consists of three sections -Verbal Ability & Reading Comprehension (VARC), Data Interpretation & Logical Reasoning (DILR), and Quantitative Aptitude (QA) -with a total of approximately 68 questions to be attempted in 120 minutes (40 minutes per section). The exam is conducted as a computer-based test by the Indian Institutes of Management (IIMs).

The CAT 2026 (Common Admission Test) is the gateway for admission to prestigious MBA programs at IIMs and other top B-schools in India. It is a computer-based test (CBT) with fixed sectional time limits and no option to switch sections.

rows: +3 for correct, -1 for wrong MCQ, 0 for TITA/Unattempted

sections.heading: DILR Syllabus for CAT Exam (Data Interpretation and Logical Reasoning)
QA Syllabus for CAT Exam (Quantitative Aptitude)

content.text: Note: The CAT syllabus remains largely unchanged every year. The majority of questions in QA come from Arithmetic and Algebra.

sections.heading: Preparation Tips and Strategy for CAT 2026

items: Start early and focus on building concepts + speed.
Practice previous year papers and mocks regularly.
Improve reading speed for VARC and calculation speed for QA.
